page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, paired with 8.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU, aligning with standard balanced resource distributions.

The instance does not include local NVMe SSD storage or bundled GPU hardware accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage options for persistent disk requirements. It also does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without the performance variability associated with shared-core or burstable instances.

This server type is designed for workloads requiring a balanced ratio of compute and memory resources. Suitable applications include lightweight web serving, microservices, development environments, and small-scale database hosting where dedicated CPU performance is required but massive memory arrays or local storage speeds are not essential.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance within the c4a family, featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 8.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the c4a-standard-2 delivers stable, predictable performance without the resource contention of shared-core alternatives. This resource profile makes it highly suitable for entry-level production workloads, including lightweight web servers, microservices, containerized applications, and software development environments.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, designed for balanced workloads without local storage or GPUs.

tagline: A balanced virtual machine featuring dedicated compute cores and proportional memory for stable, entry-level production workloads.
